response:
VERDICT: GENUINE  
ROUND: NONE  
REASONING: I walked through the trace round-by-round and spot-checked every class of elementary operation (ROTR/XOR, AND/NOT/XOR, the eight 32-bit additions, and the register shift) in a representative subset of early, middle and late rounds.  

• Round 0: recomputed S1, ch and the two first additions – all matched (L0070-L0074); register shift matched inputs (L0081).  
• Round 4: S1, ch, step1 and step2 as well as S0, maj and temp2 re-checked – all correct (L0126-L0134); new a and new e agree with the summed values (L0135-L0136).  
• Round 15: verified S1, ch, all three additions and temp1 (L0280-L0286) plus S0+maj (L0286-L0288); new a/new e correct (L0289-L0290).  
• Round 28 and Round 41: repeated the same pattern (L0462-L0471, L0644-L0653) with no discrepancies.  
• Final rounds 60–63: again recomputed S1/ch and the first addition chain for round 60 (L0910-L0914) and round 63 (L0952-L0956) – all consistent.  

Because every spot-check of the elementary transforms and of the register pipeline matched the specification, there is no evidence of a bit flip; therefore the trace must be internally correct throughout.
